hover: Total cash flow into the University
Resources that the University cannot spend however it wants due to donor specifications.
Private gifts that come with strings attached: for example, specifying that funds must be used for a particular program.
Profit gained from investing the endowment that must be used in ways pre-specified by donors.
An amount withdrawn from the endowment that must be used in ways pre-specified by donors.
The financial statement does not specify what this category includes.
Resources that the University can spend however it wants.
All revenue from student tuition after financial aid is subtracted.
Grants and contracts for research and other activities from local, state, and/or federal government.
Private gifts that do not come with strings attached, in addition to grants and contracts from private organizations.
Profit gained from investing the endowment that can be used in any way the University wishes.
An amount withdrawn from the endowment that can be used in any way the University wishes.
Includes auxiliaries, other income, and net assets released from restrictions.
